item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article ticks a lot of boxes for me.

I'm a sucker for a superhero.
I like SCPs that are 'inexplicable' and 'odd' quite a bit.
The footage found at the end provides a nice happy ending, showing that Cactusman has done some notable good. ("I made a difference to that one." and all that)

And Cactusman is generally a good natured guy with less than optimal powers in a setting with things like the Scarlet King and SCP-682 out there.

I like it. Take my +1 and give me "Cactusman Begins"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The reason MFT Omega-7 failed is because Cactusman wasn't part of the team.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We all want to feel useful. I was also bullied when I was little so I understand the desire to protect others (and failing). I completely empathize with this guy, and that means the writing is well done. Congrats.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heartwarming, adorable, yet also sad. +1 for Cactusman, he just wants to help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can imagine him working in reconnaissance for Alpha-9 and interrogating cacti owned by people and groups of interests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my goodness, this one is just adorable. The final part made my heart melt. WE NEED MOAR CACTUSMAN! Maybe a Cactusman tale?

+1, I'd +2 if I could.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Ah, cactusman. There's a lot this article can work off and do, so here we go. (This crosslink proposal is mostly "cactus skips: a review")

The first thing I see is that Cactusman claims to be able to speak to cacti. That's a pretty bold claim, right? If only there were some way that the Foundation could possibly verify this claim?

This:

  • Empathic connections with other members of the Cactaceae family. Through testing, it has been demonstrated that SCP-2800's metabolic rate increases significantly while it is communicating with cacti. Said cacti are noted to increase water and nutrient absorption from the soil they are planted in while SCP-2800 is communicating with them.

To:

  • Empathic connections with other members of the Cactaceae family. Through testing, it has been demonstrated that SCP-2800's metabolic rate increases significantly while it is communicating with cacti. Said cacti are noted to increase water and nutrient absorption from the soil they are planted in while SCP-2800 is communicating with them. SCP-2800 has been able to communicate with SCP-3388 without a cell phone and SCP-2497 without the standard light code.

Additionally, this is cactusman. He seems to be mostly cooperative with the Foundation, and at the very least, they'd want to test some stuff with him, like other cactus anomalies. So, I suggest linking to all the other cactaceous scps on the site (there aren't that many) in a new paragraph at the end of the description:

Further crosstesting of SCP-2800 with other cactaceous anomalies — such as SCP-1277, SCP-1478 and SCP-822 — has been proposed and is currently pending approval.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.
Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really love this.

really really really

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anyway, I'm gonna hug a cactus now.

Sounds painful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will always wonder why he did not get offered into Alpha-9, I guess he was the hero the city asked for but not the one they needed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two minor nitpicks:

1. The information about him being allowed to have potted cacti, in my opinion, should be in the Containment Procedures.

2. The information about his height, weight, hair color and eye color strikes me as awfully irrelevant due to the fact that none of them are anomalous for an adult human male of European descent.

However, these issues are mild and don't bring down the article. Overall, this is a great read, and Cactusman is an adorable dork. This part in particular made my day:

SCP-2800 presents two potted cacti.

SCP-2800: They say hi, by the way. They don't talk to other people much.

Dr. Louef: What do cacti talk about?

SCP-2800: Oh, not much. They're a quiet folk. "It's bloody cold in here," "I'm thirsty," "I am a cactus," and what-not. Not much else.

Anyway, I'm gonna hug a cactus now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love him so much. Pure cactus boy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This skip is silly, cliche, and campy - just a guy with random superpowers trying to do good or whatever. And that's what I love about it. It's not often that we get a skip that's not quite wish fulfillment, yet still actually tries to do good deeds. I just like this because it's a good-quality feelgood skip, something which is unfortunately few and far between with the general tone with this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i cri eveyteim

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I am a cactus"

+1'd at that point, the sense of humor and the overall character of Cactusman is interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They had knives and stuff too. I didn't have any of that stuff. What kind of a hero uses a knife?

Poor man, if only he read The Mighty Thor instead of X-Men…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a very creative with his powers apparently. Guy should have read more comics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, hits the feels for me.

First you have a guy who keeps failing at being a hero, and the psychological results that would have.

But, he keeps on trying anyway.

Then you learn that he succeeded, at least once.

Have my +1

item.title: HELLLLP, CACTUSMAN! SAVE ME!!
item.content:encoded:
SOME CATERPILLARS ARE NIBBLING ON MY LEAVES!!!

Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2800 presents two potted cacti

SCP-2800: They say hi, by the way. They don't talk to other people much.

Dr. Louef: What do cacti talk about?

SCP-2800: Oh, not much. They're a quiet folk. "It's bloody cold in here," "I'm thirsty,"
"I am a cactus"(…).

Überupvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone get this PAL on Alpha-9.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I second this guy being Cactus Flamenco. The ending made me smile a lot.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My feels.
You get them.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How come I never checked this before?

Upvoted. The end is incredible touching.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus christ, that ending gave me the feels, mainly because I've been diagnosed with the same "hero disorder" that SCP-2800 possesses. This made me happy to know that he was able to help people and make people happy. Upvoted hard. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat and +1.

But is he a DJ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I just noticed this. Thanks for translating Cactusman! I love seeing translations of my work, and especially when it's my favorite one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Definitely love there being a "superhero" SCP who's realistically not all that effective.

And I honestly really like the heartwarming fact that he's a real, genuine hero to at least one little boy and mom, after all the times he's just had the shit kicked out of him…but now he has to live in containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have translated this SCP to Korean branch, and Korean personnels were impressed with our hero.

Someone wrote even a tale that a researcher who expresses contempt about Cactusman, thinking "There cannot be damn superhero." Then, that researcher get killed in a car accident while he saves a kid from car. Well, certainly we can say that there is no hero if everyone can be hero.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wouldn't it be cool if he could shape shift into a cactus?

EDIT: Looking back on it, that would kind of undermine the whole point of him having really bad super powers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
heart-warmed. :'(
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Gimmie a cactus, gimmie a man! Put 'em together, whadda ya got? Mancactus!"
"No, you idiot, it's Cactusman! Mancactus lives in Arizona!"
"…oh."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I spent 3 days trying to get my account working just because i wanted to upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor Cactusman :(

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The paper was the kid asking for an autograph, because that's what little kids would ask of superheroes. As for the rest, Idunno.

Edit: Derp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Coming this summer to a cactus near you…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When will Cactusman be in theaters?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read the beginning of the last log and thought, "Oh God, no." I was pleasantly surprised. Here, take an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting and relatable character. Also not overpowered.
+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice realistic (for SCP standards) superhero. I also especially like the fact that the Foundation behaves humanely toward him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This ones really good. I like the fact that this one is very touching, not just based on horror and that like the ones I've read so far on here. Grand change of pace for me so far, mate!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Internet cookie for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe the child wanted cactusman's autograph

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh… I'm probably being stupid here, but what was the paper?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

I really liked this SCP. We have so many SCPs that have supernatural powers and are basically gods… and we have this guy, a man who was blessed with anomalous powers and just wants to live out his fantasies and protect innocent civilians, but can't. He's just not good enough, which usually isn't a theme in SCPs. It's a very nice theme, though, and it invokes a great emotional response, which is always what I look for in a good SCP.
The only thing I disliked was the fact that the cacti experienced increased nutrient absorption and all that when spoken to. I think it'd be better if that was removed from his powers and replaced in the description with something along the lines of, "SCP-2800 has been frequently observed conversing with specimens of Carnigiea gigantea. During these conversations, SCP-2800's metabolism levels increase by __%. Specimens that SCP-2800 converse with do not show signs of attempting communication." In my opinion, rewriting it that way makes Cactusman seem a lot more depressed and lonely. Is he really talking to cacti, or is he just so broken from his failures that he resorts to personifying plants to keep himself sane?
On the subject of talking to cacti, it says that forming empathetic connections with cacti is one of Cactusman's anomalous powers. Anyone can form empathetic connections with anything. Maybe word it a bit differently if you do decide to keep it.
Other than that, it's wonderful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cactus McCoy, is that you? Or is it just a member of the Singing Cacti Amigos? Either way, you never fail to amuse me, wei. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On one hand, I like the presence of Cactusman on the site. I want him here.

On the other hand, does this -really- need containment? Were it not for his ability to communicate with cacti, this might be something that we could conceivably, possibly, maybe understand outside of an anomalous context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing how the most accurate picture of realistic vigilantism is painted using a guy with actual supernatural abilities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It'd be a bit more believable if they assigned him to a site in a desert and made him think he was indispensable. It'd make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, I liked this. I like the concept of a not-particularly-good superhero (even if Mark Millar and John Romita Jr. did it most famously), and I like the idea of a cactus man.

I wasn't entirely sure what was going on at the end, though. Was that a success for Cactusman? I didn't get the significance of the paper.

Also, I'm not sure why you made him Scottish. He doesn't sound particularly Scottish (he needs more 'mate' or 'pal' in his vernacular), and it seems particularly unfitting for a Scot to become cactoid - Scotland's not particularly known for its deserts!

But now, the meat of my comment: a line-by-line! There's a few little things here and there which I feel could be changed a bit to really polish up this article. Also, as a general point, although your overall grammar is fine, I'd recommend making some sentences a little bit longer to avoid duplication of sentence subjects. Colons in the list of anomalous abilities would be nice.

Line-by-line
Cactusman, Cactusman, does a few things in a  manner vaguely similar to that in which a cactus can

SCP-2800 possesses an expanded genome

Hot on the heels of him possessing brown hair and green eyes, this feels a little jarring. Simply 'has' would be fine.

SCP-2800 also has many anomalous characteristics that are consistent with the natural physical properties of the Carnegiea gigantea plant, more commonly known as the saguaro cactus.

As you mentioned 'saguaro cactus' in the previous sentence, it seems odd to then go to Linnaean name, and then back to common name. I'd recommend that on the first mention you give the Linnaean name and the common name in brackets afterwards, and thereafter refer to it as either 'C. gigantea' or 'saguaro cactus' each successive time you need to.

The anomalous capacity to tolerate above average temperatures and above average aridity

Above-average should be hyphenated. Also, you're missing a full stop at the end of this sentence.

and intervene in situations,

You could consider defining these situations more clearly, especially as you then say 'such situations'.

How did you acquire your…abilities,

You should put a space after an ellipsis (you may also optionally place one before, unless you are ending a sentence with one, in which case you should not). Also, in some places you have put four dots, where an ellipsis should only be three.

But don't take that to mean I didn't like it! I think it's great!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do I smell a foundation favorite here? I think I do.

That aside, I enjoyed the article. Kind of a realistic point of view on what a superhuman might really be able to do in the real world. You don't just get powers like laser eyes. Cactus powers are just as good, if not better in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was sad, funny and heart warming. I liked how you make us wonder if he really talks to cactus or is just really lonely and not quite sane.

I salute Cactus Man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if he would set off SCP-822? Would he be further affected by SCP-1478? So many questions!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always liked Cactusman, and I do think that this version is a significant improvement. I doubt that it's different enough to radically change the way people will vote on it, but who knows?

From a more general standpoint,I like the idea that the Foundation has at least one actual superhero in containment, and I like that he isn't ridiculously overpowered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cactus Flamenco is back! Have my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obligatory.
